## Decoding

As an extension to the main ERP analysis, we examined whether feedback outcome (win vs. loss) could be decoded from feedback-locked EEG activity in the mid-high and high-high contexts. Decoding was performed on epochs derived from the same preprocessing pipeline used for the main analysis. To preserve trial-wise alignment between EEG epochs and behavioral information, early familiarization trials were excluded after feedback epochs had been matched to the behavioral table. Thus, the decoding analysis used the same overall preprocessing framework as the main pipeline, with a slightly different timing of early-trial exclusion to ensure accurate alignment of behavioral context and outcome labels.

For the single-window analysis, decoding was conducted on multichannel EEG data within the 240-340 ms time window. This window was selected to match the canonical FCz RewP interval used in the main ERP analysis, although the decoder itself used information from all included EEG channels rather than FCz alone.
Classification was implemented with logistic regression using stratified cross-validation, and performance was quantified with ROC AUC, where chance performance corresponds to 0.50. This analysis tested whether outcome information could be reliably discriminated within the RewP-motivated time range.

A complementary time-resolved decoding analysis was then performed to characterize when outcome information became decodable over the course of the feedback epoch. Using the same classifier, decoding performance was estimated across time and then summarized with reference to the same 240-340 ms window for direct comparison with the single-window analysis. Conceptually, the two analyses address related but distinct questions: the single-window analysis tests whether outcome information is present within the predefined RewP interval, whereas the time-resolved analysis shows when that information emerges and whether its temporal profile differs across contexts. Together, these decoding analyses were treated as exploratory extensions to the main ERP findings rather than as the primary test of the study hypotheses.


## Decoding Results
